events.description: Land Operations Conference held in the Aberdeen Area, June 13-15, 1955, where committee on reporting systems was appointed
Committee on land operations reporting appointed at Land Operations Conference; preliminary report made to conference on June 14
Federal Register Vol. 20, No. 155, page 5777 published amendment to 25 CFR 71.27 deleting reference to deposit of fees for submarginal land grazing privileges

events.location: Pine Ridge, Rosebud, Crow Creek, Lower Brule Reservations
events.description: Range inventory/survey conducted by Soil Conservation Service personnel under T.C.B.I.A. agreement; now considered obsolete due to radical change in concept of scientific range inventory
Rex Carey, Agency Soil Scientist at Standing Rock, transferred to Pine Ridge with headquarters at Martin
Bureau memorandum issued regarding fee patent policy for competent applicants, generating numerous telegrams, letters, and postal cards of objection or inquiry
Fee patent policy statement transmitted to North Cheyenne Superintendent by Billings Area Office
Fee patent policy statement transmitted to North Cheyenne Superintendent by Billings Area Office
Copy of Office memorandum of 11/22/55 re fee patent policy transmitted to Superintendent, North Cheyenne
Office memorandum issued re fee patent policy set forth in memorandum of May 16
Central Office memorandum issued regarding disposition of revenue from submarginal lands
Billings Area Office memorandum issued to Superintendents regarding disposition of revenue from submarginal lands
Memorandum from Chief, Branch of Realty to Area Directors regarding requirement to cite existing rights of way in patent requests
Billings Area Office memorandum to all Superintendents regarding rights of way involved in issuance of patents in fee
Billings Area Office memorandum regarding issuance of patents and deeds subject to collection of unpaid irrigation assessments
Committee reports from Aberdeen Area Land Operations Conference distributed to all Superintendents in Billings Area for comment

financial_transactions.description: Grazing fees collected for submarginal land grazing privileges previously held in special deposits, now to be deposited to Treasury General Fund Receipt Account 1719, Rental of Land Not Otherwise Classified
Unpaid irrigation assessments including construction, operation and maintenance charges required to be collected before land sale transactions finalized per Act of March 7, 1928 and 25 CFR 151

financial_transactions.amount: 20% of lease rental or permit grazing fee annually
financial_transactions.description: Recommended annual investment in conservation improvements equivalent to 20% of lease rental or permit grazing fee
Delinquent operation and maintenance charges deducted from land sale proceeds per bid form 5-114

correspondence.sender_title: Chief, Branch of Realty, Bureau of Indian Affairs
correspondence.recipient: Area Directors, Portland, Aberdeen, California, Billings, and Minneapolis
correspondence.subject: Request for issuance of patents in fee - requirement to cite existing rights of way
correspondence.action_requested: Specify applicable laws and approval dates for easements and rights of way in all patent requests; ensure information is included in transmittals
correspondence.outcome: Memorandum forwarded by Billings Area Office to all Superintendents on January 6, 1955

correspondence.sender_title: Area Director (acting), Bureau of Indian Affairs
correspondence.subject: Rights of way involved in issuance of patents in fee
correspondence.action_requested: Obtain necessary information relating to rights of way and include in transmittals with patent applications or when Indian lands advertised for public sale are traversed by approved rights of way
correspondence.outcome: Attached copy of December 13, 1954 Office letter

correspondence.sender: Office of the Area Director, Billings
correspondence.recipient: All Superintendents and Officers in Charge
correspondence.subject: Issuance of patents and deeds subject to collection of unpaid irrigation assessments
correspondence.action_requested: Establish satisfactory checking procedure to ensure compliance with irrigation assessment requirements before land sale transactions are finally completed; obtain payment for accrued charges or contract obligating purchaser to pay construction charges per 25 CFR 151
correspondence.outcome: Directive issued following Bureau auditors calling attention to failures

correspondence.subject: Issuance of patents in fee to competent applicants
correspondence.action_requested: All Bureau employees processing or reviewing fee patent applications to be guided by policy in May 16 memorandum and clarifying statement; pending exception applications to be returned to Superintendent for further action
correspondence.outcome: Temporary manual release to be issued incorporating substance of memorandum and statement

correspondence.recipient: Superintendent and Officers in Charge, Billings Area
correspondence.subject: Disposition of revenue from submarginal lands
correspondence.action_requested: Deposit any funds held in special deposits to Treasury General Fund Receipt Account 1719 before end of calendar year; hereafter deposit submarginal land revenues to appropriate general fund revenue receipt account
correspondence.outcome: Directive issued quoting Central Office memorandum of November 23, 1955

correspondence.sender: Billings Area Office, Branch of Realty
correspondence.subject: Fee patent policy memorandum of 11/22/55
correspondence.action_requested: For information - copy of Office memorandum of 11/22/55 re fee patent policy set forth in memorandum of May 16 and subsequent policy statements of June 3 and October 4, 1955

correspondence.subject: Committee reports, Land Operations Conference, Aberdeen Area
correspondence.action_requested: Review attached committee reports from Land Operations Conference held in Aberdeen Area at Sioux City, Iowa, June 13-15, 1955 and provide comments on conclusions reached by several committees
correspondence.outcome: Reports distributed with request for comment

correspondence.sender: Central Office, Bureau of Indian Affairs
correspondence.recipient: Billings Area Office and field staff
correspondence.subject: Disposition of revenue from submarginal lands - amendment to 25 CFR 71.27
correspondence.action_requested: Deposit funds held in special deposits to Treasury; hereafter deposit revenues to general fund revenue receipt account
correspondence.outcome: Quoted in Billings Area memorandum of November 29, 1955

legislative_actions.outcome: Act of March 7, 1928 (45 Stat. 210) enacted, creating first lien against irrigable lands on Indian irrigation projects for unpaid construction, operation and maintenance costs; directs lien to be recited in any patent or instrument issued for such lands

Amendment to 25 CFR 71.27 published in Federal Register Vol. 20, No. 155, page 5777, deleting reference to deposit of fees for submarginal land grazing privileges in special deposits; funds to be deposited to Treasury
Pending legislation to transfer submarginal lands and fees to respective tribes; special deposits were held in support of this legislation but amendment removed requirement pending legislative action
